content: The intrinsic reward signal from curiosity-driven methods like ICM and RND can be repurposed as a dynamic selection metric for active learning agent memory, where under-explored past trajectories are assigned higher intrinsic reward to be re-sampled for further policy training instead of discarding low-reward experiences. This creates a self-reinforcing memory curation loop that outperforms static SOTA memory retention rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
